evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Write production-grade SQL; data quality checks; dimensional modeling; ensure data quality, lineage, and documentation; Solid statistics and probability fundamentals; discipline to distinguish real signal from noise
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour: quality, testing, statistics rigor, and documentation dominate; no speed/velocity language.
evidence.domain_risk: iGaming/crypto; fraud/AML investigation; bonus abuse detection; wagering behavior; financial risk metrics (GGR/NGR); compliance constraints
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: High: crypto-enabled iGaming with explicit fraud/AML/compliance signals and financial risk management (anomaly investigation, abuse detection).
evidence.data_team_maturity: Build and maintain AI-agent workflows; Partner with multiple teams; Define event schemas; Automate recurring reporting
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Early: candidate owns analytics foundation and must establish practices across multiple teams; automation and agent workflows suggest building out early capabilities.
evidence.jd_authorship: Drive the business forward through proactive analytics; Build, maintain, and iterate dashboards; Write production-grade SQL; Build and maintain AI-agent workflows; Use Python for analysis, data quality checks, ETL; Investigate anomalies; Automate recurring reporting
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Hiring manager: highly specific ownership (SQL modeling, Python automation, agent workflows, anomaly investigation, dashboard iteration, compliance analysis) shows deep domain expertise.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Growth (wallet funnels, wagering, deposit conversion); Risk/Compliance (fraud, AML, abuse); Product/CRM (LTV, churn, lifecycle marketing); Financial metrics (GGR/NGR, ARPU)
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Mixed: risk/compliance and financial metrics (risk orientation) balanced against commercial growth metrics (revenue/retention).
evidence.autonomy_level: Own the end-to-end analytics stack; Drive business forward proactively; Build AI-agent workflows; Investigate anomalies; Define event schemas
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Strategic: candidate sets direction for analytics, owns end-to-end stack, builds infrastructure, and proactively identifies business impact.
evidence.ai_role: Build and maintain AI-agent workflows that automate recurring analytics; Solid AI-collaboration craft — prompt & context engineering, spec-driven task framing, and eval-driven iteration; Built or contributed to production AI agents
evidence.ai_role_explanation: AI user: candidate expected to build and maintain AI agent workflows for analytics automation; skilled in prompt engineering and agentic flow design.
evidence.testing_framing: data quality checks; Great Expectations/Monte Carlo for data quality; Partner to define event schemas; ensure data quality, lineage, and documentation
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Responsibility: data quality ownership framed as core responsibility; candidate owns quality checks, validation, and framework application.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Investigate anomalies (bonus abuse, botting, arbitrage, suspicious wallet rings) and recommend mitigations; Fraud/AML patterns; regional compliance constraints; data governance & privacy (GDPR); discipline to distinguish real signal from noise
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: High: fraud investigation, compliance, regulatory constraints, and anomaly detection pervasive; fear is financial/reputational loss from undetected fraud.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Build and maintain AI-agent workflows; Build, maintain, and iterate dashboards; Design and analyze experiments; Automate recurring reporting
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Fix/scale: building out analytics infrastructure and automation for growing iGaming business, not starting from zero.
evidence.loss_aversion: Investigate anomalies (bonus abuse, botting, arbitrage, suspicious wallet rings) and recommend mitigations. Understand iGaming economics & KPIs and common fraud/AML patterns. Regional compliance constraints.
